name: grasshopper-workflow
description: Grasshopper 參數化建模工作流程工具。當需要通過 MCP 協議與 Grasshopper 交互、創建和管理組件、建立連接、設置參數、執行完整建模工作流程時使用。適用於：(1) 從 MMD 文件創建 Grasshopper 定義, (2) 執行 placement_info.json 工作流程, (3) 批量管理組件和連接, (4) 參數化建模的自動化流程, (5) 解析 component_info.mmd 和 part_info.mmd 文件
capabilities: Create, query, and delete Grasshopper components via MCP protocol
Establish connections between components with parameter mapping
Set Number Slider and Vector XYZ component parameters
Parse MMD files to extract components, connections, and subgraphs
Generate and execute placement_info.json workflow files
Batch create component groups with custom colors
author: AmemiyaLai
securitySummary: Legitimate Grasshopper workflow automation tool. All 527 static findings are false positives: C2 keywords are UUIDs in GUID mappings, external_commands are Ruby code examples in documentation, and weak crypto patterns are references in documentation. The tool communicates via TCP with local MCP server on port 8080, reads/writes JSON and MMD files within project directories only, and has no external network calls or data exfiltration.
valueStatement: Creating Grasshopper definitions manually is time-consuming and repetitive. This skill enables AI assistants to programmatically create components, establish connections, and execute complete parametric modeling workflows through the MCP protocol, turning design specifications into working Grasshopper definitions automatically.
limitations: Requires a running Grasshopper MCP server on localhost:8080
Cannot modify existing Grasshopper definitions directly
Only supports components with known GUIDs in the built-in mapping
Connection parameters must be specified manually

useCases.title: Automate design iteration
useCases.description: Convert design specifications into Grasshopper definitions automatically for rapid design iteration

useCases.title: Batch component placement
useCases.description: Create complex component networks from MMD files with parallel execution for efficiency

useCases.title: Enable Claude for CAD work
useCases.description: Add Grasshopper control capabilities to AI assistants for parametric design assistance

promptTemplates.title: Create simple component
promptTemplates.scenario: Add a Number Slider to Grasshopper
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Number Slider at position x=100, y=200 with component_id SLIDER_WIDTH, then set its value to 120.0 with range 0-200

promptTemplates.title: Connect components
promptTemplates.prompt: Connect SLIDER_WIDTH to DIVISION_X using source param Number and target param A

promptTemplates.title: Execute full workflow
promptTemplates.prompt: Parse component_info.mmd, generate placement_info.json, then execute it with 10 parallel workers

promptTemplates.title: Batch group creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create groups for desktop components and leg components using the parsed subgraph information with color #FF0000

bestPractices: Use component_id keys for better readability instead of raw component IDs
Set max_workers between 10-20 for parallel execution to balance speed and stability
Save component ID mapping after creation to preserve state for subsequent operations
antiPatterns: Do not attempt to create components without specifying valid GUIDs from the mapping
Avoid connecting components without specifying the correct source and target parameters
Do not skip GUID updates when using placement_info.json from different Grasshopper versions

faq.question: What is the MCP server requirement?
faq.answer: A Grasshopper MCP server must be running on localhost:port 8080. This skill connects to it via TCP sockets.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: MMD files for component definitions and JSON files for placement instructions and component ID mappings.

faq.question: How do I integrate with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Install this skill and use the provided functions to interact with Grasshopper. The skill exposes all operations through MCP tool definitions.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. All communication is local to your machine. No data is sent externally. File operations stay within the project directory.

faq.question: Why are connections failing?
faq.answer: Check that all source components exist, the component ID mapping is saved, and you have specified correct parameter names.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual Grasshopper work?
faq.answer: This skill automates repetitive tasks. You design in MMD format once, then the skill creates all components and connections automatically.
